title: Semantik Architect Frame Schema: Relational – Relation bundle
description: Schema for relation.bundle / rel.bundle frames: a bundle of multiple relation-level facts about the same subject, used for planning, ordering, and grouped realization.

frame_type.description: Stable frame identifier for relation-bundle frames.

id.description: Optional stable identifier for this bundle instance (e.g. cluster ID, source row ID, statement-group ID).

subject.description: Entity about which the bundled relation facts are stated.

items.description: Ordered list of bundled relation entries. Each entry wraps a specific relational frame plus lightweight planning metadata such as type, salience, and ordering hints.

bundle_strategy.description: Optional high-level realization/planning hint for the bundle, e.g. 'single_sentence', 'multi_sentence', 'group_by_type', 'lead_then_details'.

max_items_per_sentence.description: Optional planner hint limiting how many bundled relations should be realized in one sentence.

info_structure.description: Optional information-structure annotation for topic/focus/background behavior at bundle level.

attributes.description: Open attribute bag for bundle-level metadata useful to downstream planning or realization.

extra.description: Free-form metadata preserved for provenance, debugging, or source-specific needs.

RelationFrame.description: Underlying relation frame carried by a bundle item. Prefer one of the concrete relational frame schemas listed here. A generic object is still accepted for compatibility with partially migrated data.

oneOf.description: Compatibility fallback for relation objects not yet migrated to a dedicated schema.

relation_type.description: Coarse relation label used for ordering and planning heuristics.

salience.description: Optional normalized importance score for this relation within the bundle. Higher means more important.

order_hint.description: Optional preferred ordering hint among bundled relations. Lower values are typically realized earlier.

group_key.description: Optional grouping key used by planners to cluster related bundle items together.

optional.description: Whether this bundled relation may be dropped if realization budgets are tight.

attributes.description: Additional item-level planning or provenance metadata.
